title: On A Clear Day I Cant See My Sister

jokes.text: Happy 3:00 in the morning, everyone. In a mere five hours, we'll be gazing at Springfield's most exciting glacier, Springfield Glacier.
jokes.setup: Bus driver cheerfully announces early morning trip to see glacier
jokes.punchline: The redundant naming 'Springfield Glacier' and overly enthusiastic tone about something mundane
jokes.explanation: Decent opener with Otto's characteristic oblivious enthusiasm and the show's trademark redundant naming convention

jokes.text: Hey, check out my T-shirt. It's wicked relevant. It's part of my 'Things Suck' line of clothing.
jokes.setup: Bart proudly showing off his T-shirt for the glacier trip
jokes.punchline: The ironic enthusiasm for nihilistic clothing brand
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Bart's juvenile contrarianism packaged as a consumer product

jokes.text: So, Lise, ready for your trip? - Sure am. See ya next fall.
jokes.setup: Bus driver asking if Lisa is ready for trip
jokes.punchline: 'See ya next fall' - pun on falling and the season
jokes.explanation: Standard dad joke/pun that sets up the next joke about repetition

jokes.text: You make those same stupid jokes every field trip. You work in the business as long as I have, you're bound to repeat yourself. See ya next fall.
jokes.setup: Lisa calls out Otto for repeating jokes
jokes.punchline: Otto immediately repeats the same joke while explaining why he repeats jokes
jokes.explanation: Excellent meta-joke about repetition that demonstrates the very thing it's discussing - well-structured comedy

jokes.text: So, Edna, I wrote up some possible discussion topics for this trip. Oh, here's one. I'm still in love with you. Discuss?
jokes.setup: Seymour presents educational discussion topics
jokes.punchline: One of the 'topics' is his romantic confession
jokes.explanation: Perfect Seymour moment - his awkward formality applied to deeply personal confession

jokes.text: Our romance is in the history books and there's nothing worth highlighting.
jokes.setup: Edna rejecting Seymour's romantic overture
jokes.punchline: Educational metaphor of 'highlighting' applied to their failed romance
jokes.explanation: Sharp comeback with perfect teacher metaphor - economical and character-specific

jokes.text: The Skin-man doesn't give up that easily.
jokes.setup: Seymour being rejected by Edna
jokes.punchline: Referring to himself in third person with awkward nickname
jokes.explanation: Classic Seymour delusion - the self-appointed cool nickname reveals his desperation

jokes.text: I feel like I'm in Fitzcarraldo. That movie was flawed.
jokes.setup: Seymour making pretentious film reference about their situation
jokes.punchline: Edna's dismissive critical assessment
jokes.explanation: Good character dynamic - Seymour's pretention met with Edna's blunt dismissal

jokes.text: What happened to the glacier? It's nearly melted. This must be due to global warming. Young lady, the federal government's position on global warming is that it does not exist. The glacier's doing just fine.
jokes.setup: Lisa observing the obviously melted glacier and suggesting global warming
jokes.punchline: Park ranger denying reality while standing next to melted glacier
jokes.explanation: Good political satire showing government denial of obvious evidence

jokes.text: I have a little girl just like you at home. That's where she stays-- at home.
jokes.setup: Park ranger relating to Lisa
jokes.punchline: Reveals he keeps his daughter isolated to avoid her activism
jokes.explanation: Dark humor with excellent economy - the pause before 'at home' sells the sinister implication

jokes.text: Oh, I just love it here. So many things, and so many things of each thing.
jokes.setup: Marge expressing enthusiasm about Sprawl-Mart
jokes.punchline: Her overwhelmed, inarticulate way of describing consumer abundance
jokes.explanation: Perfect Marge moment - her innocent wonder at capitalism's excess expressed in simple terms

jokes.text: Grandpa, you're a greet grater. I mean a great greeter. Now look who's senile.
jokes.setup: Marge trying to compliment Grandpa on his greeting job
jokes.punchline: She makes the verbal mistake, then calls him senile
jokes.explanation: Good reversal joke - the pot calling the kettle black with wordplay twist

jokes.text: No... ...mes.
jokes.setup: Grandpa trying to unstick cart
jokes.punchline: Cart pulls him over, breaking his word mid-syllable as he falls
jokes.explanation: Simple physical gag with good timing - the interrupted word sells the sudden fall

jokes.text: Oh, Dad. How I'll miss your cap, your walking stick, your plaster base. I'm down here, you idiot.
jokes.setup: Homer mourning what he thinks is Grandpa's statue/memorial
jokes.punchline: Grandpa is actually fine, just separated from his greeter uniform base
jokes.explanation: Good visual misdirection - Homer's typical obliviousness to obvious reality

jokes.text: No. They're helpless without a greeting. Look at 'em.
jokes.setup: Homer saying customers will be fine without a greeter
jokes.punchline: Grandpa insisting customers are lost without someone saying hello
jokes.explanation: Grandpa's inflated sense of importance in menial job

jokes.text: Make me proud. Or at least less ashamed.
jokes.setup: Grandpa giving fatherly encouragement to Homer taking over greeting job
jokes.punchline: Lowered expectations to just 'less ashamed'
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment - Grandpa's realistic assessment of Homer with perfect economy

jokes.text: I'm gonna get a glacier postcard and send it to my nana. She'll be like, 'No way.' Score.
jokes.setup: Student excited about buying postcard
jokes.punchline: Imagining grandmother's amazement at receiving postcard
jokes.explanation: Mild joke about kid's simple excitement and imagined validation from grandmother

jokes.text: I'm going to exchange this beach towel I bought at Sequoia National Park. I learned too late that it implied tumescence.
jokes.setup: Seymour wants to return a towel
jokes.punchline: His pretentious vocabulary to describe sexual imagery on towel
jokes.explanation: Perfect Seymour - using clinical vocabulary for embarrassing situation, showing his prudishness and pretension

jokes.text: If these wise warriors could speak, what would they tell us? They left a message right here. Actually carved into the rock.
jokes.setup: Seymour building up ancient wisdom from cave paintings
jokes.punchline: Implies Bart has graffitied the cave
jokes.explanation: Good setup/payoff structure deflating Seymour's pompous historical moment with vandalism

jokes.text: Nice work, Simpson. Feel free to make a rubbing.
jokes.setup: Guide praising what he thinks is Bart's respectful academic interest
jokes.punchline: Encouraging Bart to make a rubbing of what's probably inappropriate graffiti
jokes.explanation: Adult authority figure unknowingly encouraging bad behavior

jokes.text: Welcome to Sprawl-Mart. Pimple cream in aisle five. Buenos nachos. Ask about our 'señor' discount. Hey, Rabbi. Jesus loves ya. Just kidding.
jokes.setup: Homer doing greeter job enthusiastically
jokes.punchline: Series of increasingly inappropriate greetings culminating in religious joke
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Homer's inappropriate oversharing and cultural insensitivity

jokes.text: Son, you are on fire. A million times better than that worthless old coot you replaced. That coot happens to be my father. Please continue.
jokes.setup: Manager praising Homer while insulting previous greeter
jokes.punchline: Homer reveals it's his father but wants to hear more insults
jokes.explanation: Excellent Homer moment - his selfishness overrides family loyalty for validation

jokes.text: That coot happens to be my father. Please continue.
jokes.setup: Manager insulting Homer's father
jokes.punchline: Homer's complete lack of family loyalty
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer moment - selfish but honest about family dynamics

jokes.text: How would you like to be a full-time Sprawl-Mart greeter? Is there a chance for advancement? - No. Whoo-hoo. No pressure.
jokes.setup: Job offer with question about advancement
jokes.punchline: Homer celebrating the lack of pressure from no advancement possibility
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - finding positive in what others would see as negative

jokes.text: Dying alone, huh? Terrible fate to befall any woman-- Ice Age or modern day.
jokes.setup: Museum display about Ice Age woman who died alone
jokes.punchline: Seymour making it awkwardly personal about his dating life
jokes.explanation: Classic Seymour awkwardness, making ancient history about his loneliness

jokes.text: So, is there a Mrs. Park Ranger Johnson? No, ma'am. I'm married to the glacier. But, uh, it's an open marriage. Then bring it, fool.
jokes.setup: Edna flirting with park ranger to make Seymour jealous
jokes.punchline: Ranger's metaphor about being married to glacier with 'open marriage' twist
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay turning environmental dedication into sexual innuendo

jokes.text: Oh, for goodness sake. You've moved on, have you? Well, so have I.
jokes.setup: Seymour witnessing Edna flirt with another man
jokes.punchline: Obvious lie about having moved on while clearly devastated
jokes.explanation: Standard jealous ex-boyfriend behavior for Seymour

jokes.text: Just play along.
jokes.setup: Seymour trying to make Edna jealous back
jokes.punchline: Edna has to tell her fake date to play along with jealousy scheme
jokes.explanation: Simple cringe moment showing the artificial nature of their jealousy games

jokes.text: The only gas is coming from Lisa's butt.
jokes.setup: Lisa giving serious environmental speech about greenhouse gases
jokes.punchline: Bart reduces it to juvenile fart joke
jokes.explanation: Classic Bart undermining Lisa's earnestness with lowest common denominator humor

jokes.text: Hey, whoever's doing that, get off the frequency. We're combing the woods. There's a killer loose out here. Let him do it, Chief. It lightens the mood.
jokes.setup: Police radio interference from Bart's fart sounds
jokes.punchline: Chief Wiggum allowing comedy during serious police work
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Wiggum's inappropriate priorities and Lou's enablement

jokes.text: Help. I'm sinking in the lake. You mean, you're walking on the glacier. Whatever.
jokes.setup: Bart dramatically calling for help
jokes.punchline: His dismissive 'whatever' when corrected about what he's actually doing
jokes.explanation: Good Bart moment - his indifference to accuracy when seeking attention

jokes.text: Check it out. That frog has a nerd on its butt.
jokes.setup: Bart pointing out something he sees
jokes.punchline: He's calling Lisa (stuck to frog) a 'nerd on its butt'
jokes.explanation: Typical Bart insult with visual component - calling Lisa a nerd while she's in predicament

jokes.text: What are you gonna do, tell Mom and Dad? They're powerless figureheads.
jokes.setup: Lisa threatening to tell their parents
jokes.punchline: Bart's cynical but accurate assessment of parental authority
jokes.explanation: Excellent line that's both funny and observationally accurate about family dynamics

jokes.text: Hey, there's a dog in the next car lookin' at me. Hey, dog.
jokes.setup: Lisa trying to confront Bart about his behavior
jokes.punchline: Bart completely ignoring her to focus on irrelevant dog
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Bart's ADD-like avoidance of serious conversations

jokes.text: Good morning, world. Eat my shorts. There'll be no shorts eaten today, young man.
jokes.setup: Bart using his catchphrase
jokes.punchline: Wiggum taking the idiom literally
jokes.explanation: Standard callback joke with Wiggum's typical literal interpretation

jokes.text: Restraining order? Bull spit. No piece of paper keeps me from whaling on my sister.
jokes.setup: Bart being served restraining order
jokes.punchline: His defiance using censored curse word and violent threat
jokes.explanation: Good Bart moment - his immediate dismissal of authority and casual violence threat

jokes.text: The clerk also gave me this bench warrant for Dad's arrest. Aw, damn it.
jokes.setup: Lisa explaining restraining order process
jokes.punchline: Casual revelation of Homer's legal troubles
jokes.explanation: Good escalation revealing Homer's separate legal issues as throwaway detail

jokes.text: Hey, I don't make the laws, I just... something with them.
jokes.setup: Wiggum trying to explain his role in law enforcement
jokes.punchline: Can't even remember what he's supposed to do with laws
jokes.explanation: Perfect Wiggum moment - his complete incompetence at his basic job function

jokes.text: Hi, I'm Gary Busey.
jokes.setup: Restraining order educational video beginning
jokes.punchline: Gary Busey as spokesman for restraining orders
jokes.explanation: Great casting choice - Gary Busey's real-life erratic behavior makes him perfect for restraining order video

jokes.text: Oh, man, I've been there.
jokes.setup: Video describing stalking scenario
jokes.punchline: Homer relating to the stalker behavior
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer - inappropriate identification with problematic behavior

jokes.text: Never say die. Quitters never prosper. And No means yes.
jokes.setup: Life lessons from father in restraining order video
jokes.punchline: Escalating from positive to clearly inappropriate dating advice
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from inspirational to predatory - dark comedy about toxic masculinity

jokes.text: I'm going to let you in on a little secret. Joe is me. And Mary is a composite of 12 different women and a small independent film company, all of whom couldn't deal with me, because I'm too real.
jokes.setup: Busey revealing the educational video is autobiographical
jokes.punchline: Absurd details about multiple restraining orders including from film company
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation with specific absurd details - Busey's delusion about being 'too real' is perfect

jokes.text: Don't worry, son. No one's going to make you count to 20.
jokes.setup: Bart worried about measuring 20 feet distance
jokes.punchline: Homer's assumption that Bart can't count to 20
jokes.explanation: Good Homer moment - his low expectations for Bart's abilities while trying to be reassuring

jokes.text: It'll keep your pole looking great, poke after poke after poke. Poke.
jokes.setup: Homer explaining pole cover features
jokes.punchline: Repetitive 'poke' building to actual poke of Lisa
jokes.explanation: Simple escalation joke building repetition to actual action

jokes.text: Homer, why did you put the screwdriver at the end? Show you what it's like in the real world.
jokes.setup: Marge questioning dangerous modification to pole
jokes.punchline: Homer's twisted logic about preparing kids for violence
jokes.explanation: Good dark Homer logic - his misguided attempt at life preparation

jokes.text: A.M. kindergarten made the Bart doll. P.M. kindergarten made the Lisa.
jokes.setup: Seymour explaining why Bart can't be in school
jokes.punchline: Kindergarteners have made voodoo dolls of the Simpson kids
jokes.explanation: Good absurd visual - kindergarteners crafting voodoo dolls is delightfully wrong

jokes.text: The smell is manure, and the dripping's manure.
jokes.setup: Bart asking about unpleasant conditions in groundskeeper shack
jokes.punchline: Willie's matter-of-fact explanation that everything awful is manure
jokes.explanation: Good Willie moment - his blunt honesty about awful working conditions

jokes.text: But we're already on chapter seven. Then you'll be teaching Willie.
jokes.setup: Bart pointing out he's behind in math
jokes.punchline: Willie suggesting Bart will end up teaching him
jokes.explanation: Good reversal joke acknowledging Willie's educational limitations

jokes.text: The only cure is this anti-poking potion. Oops, missed the vein. Dang, missed it again.
jokes.setup: Dr. Hibbert treating Lisa's arm injury
jokes.punchline: Multiple failed injection attempts
jokes.explanation: Standard medical incompetence gag with Dr. Hibbert

jokes.text: Sorry, I'm distracted. My housekeeper is suing me for sexual harassment. She hung the mistletoe. What was I supposed to do?
jokes.setup: Dr. Hibbert explaining his distraction
jokes.punchline: His inappropriate justification for sexual harassment
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy about professional misconduct with weak justification

jokes.text: 'Don't have a cow, man.' Yeah, he's all... 'Cowabunga, dude.' I mean, what's that mean? Nothing, I bet.
jokes.setup: Lisa and Homer discussing Bart's catchphrases
jokes.punchline: Questioning the meaning of signature character phrases
jokes.explanation: Meta joke about the show's own catchphrases being meaningless

jokes.text: If you don't, we'll ship you right back to Mexico. But I'm a U.S. citizen. How sure of that are you?
jokes.setup: Manager threatening Apu with deportation
jokes.punchline: Making him doubt his own citizenship
jokes.explanation: Dark workplace exploitation humor with immigration anxiety

jokes.text: My sister has no sense of humor. I'm sure you understand. It's why you became a judge instead of finding a husband.
jokes.setup: Bart defending himself to judge
jokes.punchline: Insulting the judge with sexist stereotype
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart - making situation worse by insulting authority figure

jokes.text: My sister has no sense of humor. I'm sure you understand. It's why you became a judge instead of finding a husband.
jokes.setup: Bart explaining his side to the judge
jokes.punchline: Insulting the judge's personal life while asking for leniency
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart moment - completely misreading the situation and making it worse

jokes.text: I have a husband. What is he, blind and deaf?
jokes.setup: Judge correcting Bart about having husband
jokes.punchline: Bart doubling down with more insults
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation - Bart can't help but make things worse

jokes.text: That's 61 meters. Sacré bleu.
jokes.setup: Judge increasing restraining order to 200 feet
jokes.punchline: Random French person translating and reacting dramatically
jokes.explanation: Random absurd interjection that adds international perspective to legal proceeding

jokes.text: Why should I go inside? The world is my toilet now.
jokes.setup: Homer telling Bart to go inside to use bathroom
jokes.punchline: Bart's crude philosophy about outdoor living
jokes.explanation: Good Bart line showing his adaptation to exile with crude wisdom

jokes.text: I slept on the grill.
jokes.setup: Homer complaining about working all night
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of sleeping on restaurant grill
jokes.explanation: Great throwaway line revealing Homer's past workplace sleeping arrangements

jokes.text: I've never worked all night, never. And I worked at an all-night diner for three years. I slept on the grill.
jokes.setup: Homer complaining about working all night
jokes.punchline: Revealing he slept through his previous overnight job literally on the cooking surface
jokes.explanation: Excellent Homer backstory that perfectly explains his work ethic and safety awareness

jokes.text: That's it, mister. I am disgruntled. And up until now, I was relatively gruntled.
jokes.setup: Homer getting angry at workplace abuse
jokes.punchline: Playing with prefix to create 'gruntled' as opposite of disgruntled
jokes.explanation: Excellent wordplay showing Homer accidentally creating linguistic insight while angry

jokes.text: And with no brain damage-amage-amage-amage-amage-amage.
jokes.setup: Homer claiming he successfully removed chip with no brain damage
jokes.punchline: Stuttering repetition proving brain damage occurred
jokes.explanation: Perfect irony - the stuttering immediately contradicts his claim about brain damage

jokes.text: Homer, we took our chips out years ago. Really? Then why do you still work here? We've learned to accept the things we cannot change... and steal everything that's not nailed down.
jokes.setup: Apu explaining they removed control chips long ago
jokes.punchline: Philosophical acceptance paired with systematic theft
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark comedy mixing serenity prayer wisdom with criminal behavior

jokes.text: We've learned to accept the things we cannot change... and steal everything that's not nailed down.
jokes.setup: Philosophical acceptance of workplace conditions
jokes.punchline: Combining wisdom with petty theft as coping mechanism
jokes.explanation: Excellent subversion of inspirational rhetoric into workplace crime justification

jokes.text: Well, I mean, it's a little American-primitive, but who am I to dismiss outsider art?
jokes.setup: Lisa seeing Bart's crude sculpture of her
jokes.punchline: Her intellectual analysis of clearly amateur work
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lisa moment - applying high art criticism to brother's stick figure

jokes.text: Are you putting feces on the tip? No, I'm giving it up.
jokes.setup: Bart suspicious of Lisa's motives with the pole
jokes.punchline: His assumption she would weaponize it with feces
jokes.explanation: Good Bart paranoia showing his expectation of escalating warfare

jokes.text: Oh, man. This is every effigist's worst nightmare.
jokes.setup: Bullies about to burn Lisa effigy when real Lisa appears
jokes.punchline: Jimbo using pretentious term 'effigist' for their crude activity
jokes.explanation: Excellent vocabulary joke - juvenile bully using sophisticated art term

jokes.text: Well, you see, the fire represents... your musical ability?
jokes.setup: Bart trying to explain burning the statue
jokes.punchline: Weak attempt to make it metaphorical rather than destructive
jokes.explanation: Good Bart moment - his desperate attempt to reframe obvious malice

jokes.text: Oh, Bart, I missed your lies.
jokes.setup: Lisa forgiving Bart after his weak excuse
jokes.punchline: Acknowledging that she actually missed his dishonesty
jokes.explanation: Sweet character moment acknowledging their dysfunctional but loving relationship

tmdb_overview: Lisa does what every little sister has dreamed of - she gets a restraining order put on her big brother. Bart is forced to live in the back garden, but soon realises the joy of communing with nature.
